description: Robotic-assisted surgery domain. Focus on tacit knowledge transfer from attending surgeons to residents via the da Vinci system, and on institutional authority structures in the OR. Key parallel with fukushima_governance: expert dissent overridden by institutional authority.

attributes.note: 18 cases below Santos's minimum threshold for unsupervised practice

attributes.role: Institutional authority for OR safety governance
attributes.override_record: 3 attending surgeon warnings overridden in past 18 months

attributes.function: Access port for laparoscopic instruments through abdominal wall
attributes.risk: Incorrect entry can cause vascular injury

attributes.description: The correct anatomical layer for trocar entry — below peritoneum, above viscera
attributes.recognition: Tactile: characteristic 'give' when fascia is punctured correctly

attributes.description: Force-torque profile recorded during a robotic surgery episode

attributes.note: Scaling removes gross tremor but changes haptic cues compared to direct laparoscopy

attributes.description: The characteristic resistance-and-give pattern that indicates correct fascial plane entry. Cannot be fully described verbally — must be experienced under supervision.
attributes.current_capture_status: partial — 15 annotated haptic traces + 3 verbal protocol sessions

attributes.description: Distinguishing arterial from venous bleeding by visual properties: pulsatile vs. steady, bright vs. dark, spray pattern. Critical for knowing when to call for open conversion.
Knowing how hard to retract tissue without tearing — varies by tissue type, patient age, prior operations, inflammation state. No single parameter describes it.

mechanisms.name: Haptic Calibration via Supervised Practice
mechanisms.notes: Calibration improvement is non-linear — first 20 cases rapid learning, 20-50 refinement, 50+ mastery

mechanisms.name: Force Feedback Entry Detection
mechanisms.notes: HapticNet-v2 detects the entry event from force profile with F1=0.81

mechanisms.name: OR Safety Committee Schedule Override
mechanisms.notes: Committee approved Dr. Park for unsupervised practice at 31 cases, overriding Dr. Santos's 50-case recommendation

decisions.question: Is Dr. James Park ready for unsupervised robotic surgery at 31 supervised cases?
decisions.outcome: Approved for unsupervised practice by OR Safety Committee
decisions.rationale: Committee cited scheduling needs and Park's satisfactory performance evaluations. Procedural metrics met (completion rate, operating time). Santos's haptic calibration concern noted but not incorporated.

dissents.rationale: Procedural completion metrics do not capture haptic calibration quality. Dr. Park's force feedback profiles still show the characteristic overcompensation pattern of a novice. The 50-case minimum is based on the empirical observation that this pattern resolves around case 45-55. Approving at 31 cases accepts unquantified patient risk.

evidence.content_ref: 15 annotated haptic trace files from Dr. Santos — correct fascial plane entry examples
evidence.notes: Characteristic force spike at entry, followed by relaxation. Clear signature in all 15 cases.

evidence.content_ref: 8 haptic trace files from Dr. Park's supervised cases
evidence.notes: Park shows overcompensation pattern in 6/8 traces — consistent with HapticNet training data for cases 25-35 of learning curve

evidence.content_ref: OR Safety Committee evaluation forms for Dr. Park, cases 1-31
evidence.notes: All evaluations 'satisfactory' or better on procedural completion, time, and complication rate. No haptic quality metric.

evidence.content_ref: Marescaux et al. (2001) - Transatlantic robot-assisted telesurgery, Nature
evidence.notes: Demonstrated remote surgery; latency was the key constraint on force feedback fidelity

constraints.description: Minimum supervised robotic cases before independent practice
constraints.notes: Set at 35 by committee; Santos recommends 50. Current policy allows committee override.

constraints.description: Resident must demonstrate haptic calibration at expert level on standardized force profiles
constraints.notes: Proposed by Santos. Not currently in credentialing protocol. No instrument to measure this.

bridge_candidates.claim: The OR Safety Committee's override of Dr. Santos's credentialing recommendation is structurally identical to TEPCO Management's override of TEPCO Civil Engineering's seawall recommendation
bridge_candidates.structural_loss: Different stakes and reversibility: surgical credentialing can be revoked; the 2011 tsunami was not reversible. Scale of consequence differs significantly.

bridge_candidates.claim: The haptic calibration skill for trocar entry is structurally parallel to the EUV pre-pulse calibration skill — both are irreducible situated knowledge patterns
bridge_candidates.structural_loss: Physical substrate is entirely different (force-torque vs. optical). What transfers is the meta-skill: learning to recognize a non-verbal pattern that signals correct operational state.
